observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:
Petty Cash Custodian to immediately locate and submit the documents to the Municipal Accountant in order to liquidate the ₱180,000.00; and
Municipal Accountant to use the proper journal entries in recording replenishment of petty cash fund in accordance with pro-forma accounting entries prescribed in Section 52 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
We also recommended that Management discontinue using PCF to facilitate cash payment to AICS beneficiaries and grant cash advances to designated Special Disbursing Officer for this particular program.

We recommended that Management:
send letters to the concerned officers and employees demanding the immediate settlement/liquidation of their outstanding cash advances through the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports and enforce the withholding of salary, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 if no action is taken after the lapse of the timeline set in the demand letter;
stop the granting of additional cash advance for travel and training to personnel with unliquidated cash advances unless the previous cash advance/s is/are fully settled; and
monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
only approve Disbursement Voucher pertaining to cash advance if the required supporting documents and its purpose are in keeping with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
facilitate the preparation and submission of the complete Liquidation Reports of the cash advances noted above to COA for audit and verification.

We recommended that Management:
allocate budget for the registration of the Land recognized in the financial statements; and
initiate the processes in securing the transfer certificate of titles (TCT) of the purchased lots to support claims of ownership to fully protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Municipal Accountant to dig from previous years’ records the details and information needed to ascertain the correctness and completeness of the beginning balances of all the LGUs infrastructure assets;
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers, lapsing schedules, complete PPELC and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements to support the balance of the Road Network System (1-07-03-010) account; and
General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in conducting the inventory of all local roads and comply with the records and reports required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
cause for the immediate remittance of the past due taxes withheld;
maintain and consistently update the subsidiary ledger for Due to BIR (2-02-01-020) account to properly reflect the balance for monthly remittance; and
facilitate the monthly remittance in full and within the prescribed period of all taxes to be withheld to avoid possible penalties.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting documents totaling ₱19.38 million to give the Audit Team the opportunity to  review the transactions and to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the necessary administrative action to enforce the timely submission of the monthly reports and accounts from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to the Office of Municipal Accountant and eventually to the Audit Team in compliance with Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to take-up the necessary accounting entries to recognize the check disbursement made and the expenses incurred and to ensure that same error will not be committed in the future.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan consider the reduction of communication allowance being provided as well as the adoption of implementing guidelines in the grant of communication allowance to officials and employees;
the Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims for communication allowances are supported with certification signed by the agency head or his duly authorized representative that the amounts claimed are used for official purposes only; and
Management consider the use of mobile phone fixed plan offered by telecommunication networks and that any amount in excess of the plan amount shall be charged against the personal accounts of the claimant.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all documentary requirements for claims on travelling allowances/expenses are complied with before payments are made.

We recommended that Management:
require the different offices/end-users in the agency to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for the year and have them duly evaluated and reviewed for consolidation into a comprehensive, workable and realistic Annual Procurement Plan (APP) which would reflect the details of the procurement activities that the agency seeks to undertake within the calendar year;
strictly follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in their Annual Procurement Plan; and
ensure compliance with Section 10 of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, that all procurement shall be undertaken through competitive bidding as the default mode or primary method of procurement so that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We recommended that Management inquire and clarify with the GPPB the possibility of belated posting in the GPPB online portal of purchases through emergency procurement during CY 2020.
It is further recommended to prospectively comply with the posting requirements in the procurement law to ensure transparency in all procurement activities in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local School Board include in the ensuing year’s appropriation, programs/projects/activities related to Early Childhood Care and Development in compliance to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1, series of 2017.
We recommended that Management prepare a catch-up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to adopt measures and work hand in hand to intensify tax collection efforts in order to maximize the collections of the Municipality and meet its projected revenue in order to fund its development projects.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the prescribed supporting documents before granting cash advances to accountable officers to avoid possible misuse/misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
direct the Accountant, GSO and Requisitioning Party/Office to strictly adhere with the required flow of transactions and accomplish/update records/reports in processing procurement and requisition of supplies and materials for proper monitoring, recording and accounting of delivered and issued/consumed/distributed inventories; and
record supplies and materials deemed inventory assets as inventories first and their corresponding expense accounts once issued/consumed/distributed.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: (a) compute and record annual depreciation on depreciable properties to properly state the amount of PPE and net income in the Financial Statements; (b) regularly prepare a lapsing schedule of PPE to serve as guide in the computation of annual depreciation; and (c) prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
adhere with the required accounting and recording of PPE and assign an employee who shall prepare the supporting schedules and reports on a full -time basis, for reliable presentation and reporting of PPE accounts; and
assign an employee to coordinate with the GSO in the reconciliation of the recorded PPE with the latter’s RPCPPE and Property Cards, also on a full-time basis; and
compute and record annual depreciation on depreciable properties to properly state the amount of PPE and net income in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
adhere strictly with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445 at all times and submit/attach complete contract documents and required supporting documents to government disbursements;
submit immediately the subject/needed lacking supporting documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of the completed projects to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspension; and
refrain from approving government claims with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
strictly adhere to government’s procurement law to ensure that transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the LGU’s procurement process are observed; and
ensure that only those expenses provided for in the grant and utilization of cash advances are approved; and
submit the liquidation reports for the subject cash advances for proper review and verification.

We recommended and Management agreed to require strict adherence to Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which requires the proper supporting documents for repair and maintenance of equipment and motor vehicles.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the use of DV for all money claims against government funds or the LGU may pay honorarium, overtime pay and other related transactions through cash advance wherein in a check and the DV is in the name of the disbursing officer.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly adhere to Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (b) record transferred funds/donations as financial assistance with specific purpose to appropriate receivable accounts and require the recipients to liquidate funds received, for proper monitoring of the utilization thereof to avoid unwarranted loss/misuse of government funds.

We recommended that Management create a well-planned LDF program to minimize changes that cause unnecessary delays and prepare catch up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD; and (b) direct the Municipal Budget Office, Accounting Unit and designated GAD Focal Person to coordinate and properly monitor GAD fund utilization to guide them in the timely and prompt implementation of GAD PPAs.
